ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Ba naa kpak tu chaan Koosa wati, o zi nyɛ ba na unu a nyɛ ba tu sɔɔna.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough knowledge of the Tampulma language to provide an accurate translation of that text.

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Doone Sajusi tim ta bɔnɛ fun wa Yesu wati. Ba yaa fun a ŋɔ di nara siwɛɛ, ba wa zi bu koti a si ba sou ni.
translated: I’m sorry, I can’t translate this text.

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Zɛmɛ ta maga fun ŋɔ un, “Koosa la zi na ya wa ya nilulɛ galimsa a lɛrgi ya diŋsa o sou daha.”

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Nara nama wɛ kpak min sikirɛ, domi wuzu butora wa ba nyɛn mu wati a vɛ kpak diwe aa diili.
translated: I am unable to translate the provided Tampulma text.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Ba wara yau diwe a li yau ni, see ba te chaksi a a zi di. Ba gbanko funɛ tiŋ ba nawaalsa nyinbaan naa la biksi ba kisisi ba gbaŋsa, tempilsa awa lugɛ tempilsa a zi di.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Zɛmɛ ba naa funɛ kani a go un, o fun pɛɛsi ba, “Anti ya mu niin wuti awa mu niinbisa?”
translated: Unable to translate the provided text due to insufficient information.

ref: Can worry make you live longer?
text: Ma anti la zi gbaari o bunsa a li muksi daha?

ref: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
text: “Maa nu chenani n kaa ŋɔ ma nan. Ma doso a nu mu sɛma a gbaari, Koosa zi suŋi mau ma zim a gbaari.

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: “Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon a fun si a kaali o ŋmaha di o la zaasi kiduha.

ref: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
text: Ma si a il a kisumi Koosa, chen ni sintana wa zi wa mur ma ma tiŋ o nɔ. Ma tusa daŋni ma ya chenani mu sɛm chenu, amaa ma bara vɛ kpoki.”
